file_insights.primary_purpose: Comprehensive project documentation with MCF integration focus
key_sections: Features table for content generation agents
Platform integrations detail (TikTok Shop and Amazon SP-API MCF)
Quick start examples with code snippets
critical_additions: MCF integration section emphasizing 2-3 day shipping differentiation
Complete code examples for MCF order creation and inventory checks
Detailed environment variable requirements for Amazon SP-API
Reference to separate MCF documentation at integrations/amazon_sp_api/README.md

patterns_discovered.evidence: New file creation focused on comprehensive README with detailed sections before implementation details
patterns_discovered.significance: Ensures users understand the full system architecture and integration capabilities upfront

patterns_discovered.evidence: Multiple Python code snippets integrated throughout README for practical usage
patterns_discovered.significance: Reduces barrier to entry for developers wanting to use MCF and other integrations

patterns_discovered.evidence: Separate amazon_sp_api and tiktok_shop integration modules with independent documentation
patterns_discovered.significance: Allows developers to use integrations independently without full system dependency

patterns_discovered.evidence: Heavy reliance on .env files with example template and multi-environment support
patterns_discovered.significance: Follows production-ready security practices for credential management

patterns_discovered.evidence: Multiple references to 'Infinity Vault' brand positioning as 'Battle-Ready Equipment'
patterns_discovered.significance: Ensures all AI-generated content maintains consistent brand identity across channels

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: MCF eligibility requirement not obvious in quick start
gotchas_discovered.description: Code examples use MCF without explicitly noting that not all SKUs may be MCF-eligible, requiring pre-verification
gotchas_discovered.impact: Users might attempt orders with ineligible SKUs, causing failures
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Add note in MCF quick start example mentioning inventory eligibility check

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Token refresh complexity hidden from users
gotchas_discovered.description: Documentation states tokens are 'automatically cached and refreshed' but doesn't explain what happens on refresh failures
gotchas_discovered.impact: Users may not know how to handle authentication failures in production
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Add section on token refresh error handling and retry strategies

gotchas_discovered.description: Git diff shows file was truncated ('... (truncated, 15103 chars total)'), suggesting full README is longer than shown
gotchas_discovered.impact: Incomplete view of final documentation; additional sections may exist
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Review complete README file directly to verify all content was added

gotchas_discovered.description: Examples hardcode 'ATVPDKIKX0DER' (US marketplace) without mentioning other marketplace support
gotchas_discovered.impact: Users in non-US regions may copy examples without adjusting marketplace IDs
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Add note that marketplace IDs must be adjusted for different regions

approach_outcome.objective: Update main README with MCF integration section
approach_outcome.execution_summary: Created comprehensive 427-line README.md file for AI Content Agents project with detailed MCF integration documentation, code examples, configuration instructions, and security best practices
key_accomplishments: Established clear project overview and feature matrix
Documented MCF integration as core differentiator (2-3 day shipping via Amazon)
Provided complete setup instructions with environment variable requirements
Included practical code examples for blog generation, MCF orders, and inventory checks
Created troubleshooting section addressing common MCF errors
Referenced separate MCF documentation at integrations/amazon_sp_api/README.md
Implemented security best practices section for credential handling
approach_outcome.approach_effectiveness: Documentation-first approach successfully communicates system architecture, integration capabilities, and practical usage patterns in single comprehensive file

recommendations.recommendation: Add explicit warning in MCF examples about SKU eligibility and inventory checks before order creation
recommendations.rationale: Users may attempt orders with ineligible or out-of-stock SKUs, causing failures and confusion

recommendations.recommendation: Expand troubleshooting section with token refresh failure scenarios and recovery procedures
recommendations.rationale: Production systems need clear guidance on handling authentication failures and token expiration

recommendations.recommendation: Add marketplace ID validation helper function and document region-specific configuration
recommendations.rationale: Hardcoding marketplace IDs in examples creates support burden for non-US regions

recommendations.recommendation: Add async/await versions of code examples for high-throughput scenarios
recommendations.rationale: Current examples show synchronous patterns that may bottleneck in production batch operations

recommendations.recommendation: Document dry-run mode more prominently with explicit warnings about real order creation
recommendations.rationale: Current examples show test mode availability but may not be obvious to developers running integration tests

recommendations.recommendation: Complete review of full README file to confirm all sections render correctly given truncation in diff
recommendations.rationale: Diff output was truncated; verify all intended content was properly written to file
